steps.title: Identify the opening phrase and main clause structure
steps.content: The opening noun phrase describes a specific person: “One of the few African American global explorers during the turn of the 20th century.” After the comma, the main clause begins and needs a subject. The answer depends on which noun fills that subject position.

steps.title: Apply the matching rule
steps.content: When an opening describes a person, the main clause's subject must be that person. Choice A correctly makes Matthew Henson the subject—he is the explorer described in the opening. Choices C and D make Greenland and treks the subjects, illogically attaching the description of a person to a place and object instead. Choice B is incoherent. That matches choice A — A is correct.
